# Gecko

> Gecko is the API comprehension layer for agents. Point it at an API's
> surface (OpenAPI 3.x today) and it generates question-shaped, first-call-correct
> agent tools, handles auth and paywalls invisibly, and lets the agent call the real
> API directly for data. It is a control plane — it stores the API surface, the tool
> defs, and correctness metadata, and never response payloads, user data, or secrets.
> It composes on top of payment rails (x402) and protocols (MCP); it is not itself a
> rail or a marketplace.

Gecko's engine is the open-source `surfcall` package — the brand is Gecko; the Python
package, repo (`GeckoVision/surfcall`), and CLI you'll see in code samples are named
`surfcall`.

When an agent should reach for Gecko: you have a goal that needs an external API,
you don't have hand-written integration code, and you can't tell whether you'd be
calling the API correctly — especially for long-tail, messy, or paywalled APIs.
Gecko turns "I want X" into the right endpoint and a well-formed first call.

Honest scope: V1 (comprehension) is live end-to-end on one real, painful API (the
TxODDS World Cup API, Solana mainnet, 31+ tests), with a $0 recorded mode that runs
offline. Ingest is OpenAPI 3.x today; it does not yet work on "any" API and does not
verify the data an API returns. Multi-API correctness work is V2 and not yet live.

## Start here

- [Introduction](/introduction): what Gecko is, the three verbs (APIs get USED), and the one-path overview.
- [Quickstart](/quickstart): run the $0 recorded demo and the programmatic client — no keys, no spend.
- [Concepts](/concepts): surface, operation, question-shaped tool, session, recorded vs live, first-call-correct.

## How it works

- [How comprehension works](/comprehension): ingest → catalog → question-shaped tools → correct request, tied to the engine.
- [Recorded mode](/recorded-mode): one code path, two modes; deterministic schema → sample; $0 offline falsification.
- [Access & auth](/access-and-auth): the `Session.auth_headers()` seam, no-auth gating, and the two-token handshake.

## For AI agents

- [The MCP surface](/mcp): `list_tools` / `call_tool`, the synthetic `search_capabilities` tool, and `serve_stdio`.
- [Agent discoverability](/discoverability): intent → endpoint, hidden auth, surfacing only the calls that will work.

## Reference

- [Architecture](/architecture): control plane vs data plane, the invariants, the module map, the one API-agnostic seam.
- [Status](/status): what is live, what is early, what is deliberately deferred (V1 / V2 / V3).
